# Longline 

After the common information sections at the start of the longline form, the general section data focuses on characteristics of the special equipment and gear aboard, specifically branchlines and mitigation devices.  Branchline data details the configuration of the gear. Mitigation device data, particularly tori line data, are collect in the last section of general data. The general section is characterised by an Observed Trip ID UI.  

The next section is for set data. The first section, identified by Trip and Set ID values, has data fields for the time and location of the start and end of each set. Additional data fields characterize individual setting operations and the gear used specifically for that set. Subsequently, there are five subsections for data on specific components of a set operation: lights, mitigation measures, hooks, bait, and hauling. Each of these subsections are specifically like to observed trip IDs and set IDs. Additional data fields focus on the presence and use of each of these tools within a set. These sections conclude the data fields for setting operations.  

There are two more sections of the longline reporting form where data is identified by the unique set ID, catches and tag details. Catch data has a finer resolution, a Catch ID UI, whereby catch data is further specified. Fates and weights of each species are summarized by the data in this section. Further catch data is collected in the individual specimens and SSI sections. Each of these have UIs for further resolution, a Specimen ID UI. Specimen data includes, but is not limited to, individuals length, weight, sex, maturity, and sample collection, along with depredation data. For individuals that are SSIs, bait, leader, and depredation data are included.  

The last section of the longline form is tag details. UIs in this section continue to meet the finest resolution of unique specimen ID. Beyond reporting UIs to connect specimen data to sets within the trip, data fields include tag types, IDs, and contact details for the person that found the tag.
